region_of_origin: Singapore (Italian-inspired, served in Singapore casual dining context)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic pizza class (FOODON_00002572). No specific class exists for white-sauce mushroom pizza; closest parent match used as the most defensible available anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKW covers pizza as a category. No specific facet code for bianca/white-sauce variant exists; closest parent match used with mushroom topping noted.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for white mushroom pizza; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole 10–12 inch Bianca Fungi Pizza weighing approximately 400 g, typically shared between two people or consumed as a single meal serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza, cheese, frozen, baked (SR Legacy) and mixed mushroom composition data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for Bianca Fungi Pizza. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entries for cheese pizza (FDC ID 170720) and mixed mushroom data, cross-referenced with the input record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7). Input values are retained as the best available estimate given no direct approved-source match for this specific restaurant dish. Values are flagged as estimated. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat data are not available for this composite dish and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bianca Fungi Pizza. GI estimated at 60 (medium) based on the closest analogous food: cheese pizza on a refined wheat base, which has a reported GI of approximately 60 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). The white-sauce base and mushroom toppings do not materially alter the GI relative to the dough-dominant carbohydrate fraction. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (60) × carbohydrate per serving (75 g from 400 g × 18.75 g/100g) / 100 = 45.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 450 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g threshold, driven by mozzarella cheese and parmesan cream; saturated fat at 7.5 g/100 g contributes to arterial stiffness; a 400 g whole pizza delivers approximately 1800 mg sodium, representing 78–90% of the recommended daily sodium limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to one or two slices, request reduced cheese and lighter cream, and avoid adding salt or high-sodium condiments; pairing with a potassium-rich salad may partially offset the sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined pizza dough contributes 18.75 g carbohydrate/100 g with only 1.5 g dietary fibre/100 g, producing a medium-to-high glycaemic load (GL 45 per whole pizza); high saturated fat from mozzarella and parmesan cream may further impair insulin sensitivity with regular consumption.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit intake to one or two slices per meal, request a wholegrain or thin-crust base to reduce glycaemic load, reduce cheese quantity to lower saturated fat, and pair with a high-fibre vegetable side to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 7.5 g/100 g from mozzarella and parmesan cream exceeds the 5 g/100 g cardiovascular threshold; sodium at 450 mg/100 g elevates blood pressure risk; trans fat at 0.13 g/100 g is present at low levels; truffle oil and olive oil contribute beneficial MUFA and polyphenols that partially offset risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to one or two slices, request reduced cheese to lower saturated fat and sodium, and note that the mushroom and olive oil components provide anti-inflammatory benefit that partially offsets cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Multiple mushroom varieties (portobello, king oyster, shimeji) provide beta-glucans with evidence-based anti-inflammatory properties; truffle oil and olive oil contribute MUFA and polyphenols; however, saturated fat at 7.5 g/100 g from cheese exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA threshold and may promote pro-inflammatory cytokine activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis may consume this pizza in moderation, benefiting from the mushroom beta-glucan and olive oil polyphenol content, but should request reduced cheese to lower saturated fat below the inflammatory threshold.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bianca Fungi Pizza is a gourmet Italian-inspired white pizza served in Singapore casual dining establishments. Unlike a traditional tomato-based pizza, it uses a rich mozzarella and parmesan cream sauce as the base, topped with a medley of portobello, king oyster, and shimeji mushrooms, fragrant sage leaves, and a finishing drizzle of truffle oil. The pizza dough is oven-baked to produce a crisp, golden crust with a soft interior. The flavour profile is earthy, umami-rich, and creamy, with the truffle oil adding a distinctive aromatic depth. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 262.5 kcal, 11.25 g protein, 16.25 g fat (of which 7.5 g is saturated), and 18.75 g carbohydrate with 1.5 g dietary fibre. Sodium is elevated at 450 mg/100 g, driven by the cheese components. A whole 400 g pizza delivers approximately 1050 kcal and 1800 mg sodium. The dish is vegetarian but not vegan or gluten-free. Clinically, the high sodium and saturated fat content make it a high-concern food for hypertension and cardiovascular disease, while the refined carbohydrate base with low fibre creates a medium-to-high glycaemic load relevant to type 2 diabetes management. The mushroom varieties contribute beta-glucans with anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for rheumatoid arthritis. Portion control and ingredient modification (reduced cheese, wholegrain base) are the primary mitigation strategies for health-conscious consumers.

confidence_reason: No exact approved-source entry exists for Bianca Fungi Pizza as a named dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cheese pizza data and cross-referenced with the input record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7). GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (cheese pizza, GI ~60) per the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium because the source match is adapted rather than exact, and the input values themselves carry a 0.7 confidence rating.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite restaurant dish (white-sauce mushroom pizza) is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific composite dish are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. trans_fat_g is 0.13 g/100 g, a low but non-zero value consistent with dairy-derived trans fats from mozzarella and parmesan. All core macros (protein_g 11.25, fat_g 16.25, carbohydrate_g 18.75) are non-zero and do not require zero-macro justification. Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (cheese pizza, FDC ID 170720) as the closest approved-source analogue; input ai_generated values retained where consistent. GI estimated at 60 (medium) from analogous cheese pizza per University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008. Halal status is unconfirmed as truffle oil sourcing and cheese rennet type are unknown for this restaurant dish. Ontology match_type values set to 'closest_parent' for both FoodOn and FoodEx2, as no exact class exists for white-sauce mushroom pizza in either ontology; this corrects the previous iteration's use of 'broader' which was not in the permitted value set ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'].
